# pyffi

> pyffi is a pure Go library that calls CPython via purego — no Cgo required.
> It dynamically loads the CPython shared library at runtime and provides
> goroutine-safe bindings with automatic GIL management, full type conversion,
> async support, and uv integration for dependency management.

## Quick Start

- [README](README.md): Installation, usage examples, feature overview
- [Package Documentation](python.go): godoc with Quick Start, Type Conversions, GIL, Async, Callbacks

## Core API

- [Runtime](python.go): New(), Close(), Exec(), Eval(), RunAsync(), Import(), FromGoValue(), NewList(), NewTuple(), NewDict(), NewSet(), PythonVersion()
- [Object](object.go): Attr(), Call(), CallKw(), GetItem(), SetItem(), DelItem(), Contains(), Len(), Repr(), Compare(), Iter(), Enter(), Exit(), SetAttr(), Int64(), Float64(), GoString(), Bool(), GoBytes(), GoSlice(), GoMap(), GoValue(), Close()
- [GIL Management](gil.go): Auto-GIL (all methods acquire GIL automatically), WithGIL() for batching, GILEnsure()/Release() for manual control
- [Type Conversion](convert.go): Go ↔ Python automatic conversion — bool, int, float, string, bytes, list, tuple, dict, set, None
- [Errors](errors.go): PythonError with Type, Message, Traceback (Python 3.12+)
- [Callbacks](callback.go): RegisterFunc() — register Go functions callable from Python, with kwargs support
- [Module Binding](bind.go): Bind() for convenient module-level function calls
- [Keywords](kw.go): KW type alias for keyword arguments (map[string]any)

## Async

- [RunAsync / CallAsync](python.go): Execute async Python functions via asyncio
- [RunAsyncGo / CallAsyncGo](python.go): Non-blocking async in background goroutines
- [EventLoop](eventloop.go): Persistent asyncio event loop with goroutine integration via run_coroutine_threadsafe

## Python Discovery & Dependencies

- [Detection Logic](detect.go): Auto-detection with version filtering, symlink dedup, PYTHONHOME inference
- [macOS Detection](detect_darwin.go): Homebrew (arm64/amd64), python.org Framework, Xcode CLI Tools
- [Linux Detection](detect_linux.go): /usr/lib, /usr/local/lib, multiarch support
- [uv Integration](uv.go): WithUV(), WithUVProject(), Dependencies() — auto-install packages via uv
- [venv Cache](uv_cache.go): Hash-based cached venvs in ~/.cache/pyffi/venvs/

## Claude Agent SDK Wrapper (casdk)

- [casdk README](casdk/README.md): Full API documentation for the Claude Agent SDK Go wrapper
- [Client & Query](casdk/casdk.go): One-off queries with all ClaudeAgentOptions
- [Session](casdk/session.go): Bidirectional conversations via ClaudeSDKClient
- [Options](casdk/options.go): All QueryOption types — model, thinking, sandbox, MCP, hooks, agents, permissions, plugins, can_use_tool, SDK MCP servers
- [Message Types](casdk/message.go): Message, TextBlock, ToolUseBlock, ThinkingBlock, ToolResultBlock, Usage, ContentBlock
- [Hooks](casdk/hooks.go): Lifecycle event hooks — PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, etc. Go handlers registered as Python async callbacks
- [MCP](casdk/mcp.go): MCPTool, MCPServerConfig, SDKMCPServer, ToolAnnotations — external and in-process MCP server integration
- [Errors](casdk/errors.go): ErrCLINotFound, ErrCLIConnection, ProcessError

## Examples

- [Sessions Example](examples/sessions/main.go): List Claude Code sessions and messages
- [Query Example](examples/query/main.go): Send a query to Claude with options

## Architecture

- Auto-GIL: All public methods call autoGIL() which uses PyGILState_Ensure/Release. Nesting is safe.
- Free-threaded Python: Detected via Py_GetVersion() containing "free-threading". Skips runtime.LockOSThread.
- Reference counting: PyList_SetItem/PyTuple_SetItem steal refs. PyDict_SetItemString does NOT steal. PyList_GetItem returns borrowed refs.
- Type detection: Cached type pointers (cachedBoolType, cachedIntType, etc.) compared via PyObject_Type. Bool checked before int (subclass).
- purego binding: All C-API functions stored as function pointers on Runtime struct, registered via purego.RegisterLibFunc.

## Code Generation (pyffi-gen)

- [pyffi-gen Command](cmd/pyffi-gen/main.go): Generate type-safe Go bindings from Python modules
- [Generator](internal/gen/generator.go): Core code generation logic
- [Introspection](internal/gen/introspect.go): Python module introspection via pyffi Runtime
- [Type Mapper](internal/gen/mapper.go): Python → Go type mapping rules
- [Config](internal/gen/config.go): pyffi-gen.yaml configuration format
- [Templates](internal/gen/templates/): Go code templates for modules, classes, enums, tests
- Recommended pattern: generate into `internal/sdk/`, wrap with idiomatic public API. You can selectively wrap only what you need and mix generated bindings with inline Python code (rt.Exec/Eval) in the same package. See casdk as a practical example of this hybrid approach.

## AgentSkills

- [pyffi Skill](skills/pyffi/SKILL.md): AgentSkills-format guide for writing Go code with pyffi
- [casdk Skill](casdk/skills/casdk/SKILL.md): AgentSkills-format guide for using the Claude Agent SDK from Go

## Optional

- [CI Configuration](.github/workflows/ci.yml): GitHub Actions for macOS and Linux × Python 3.12–3.14 via uv
- [Makefile](Makefile): make ci (fmt + vet + build + test), make bench, make lint
- [Benchmark Tests](benchmark_test.go): Performance benchmarks for Exec, Call, GetItem, Iter, Eval
- [Docker Deployment](README.md): Multi-stage build examples with CGO_ENABLED=0
